title: Madring
sub: F1’s brand-new Madrid circuit: a hybrid street-and-purpose-built layout around the IFEMA exhibition centre, debuting in 2026 as the new home of the Spanish GP. 22 corners, including a rare banked section.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: A 90° Turn 1-2 sequence off the start, then a long full-throttle run that gently bends through the fast Turn 5 left kink.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: The circuit’s signature: a banked section and the dramatic "La Monumental" complex, a high-speed banked stadium-style sweep unique on the calendar.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: A technical sequence into the heavy-braking Turn 20, then the flat 90° final corners back to the line.

stratNote: A brand-new circuit with no race data. New, smooth asphalt usually means low degradation and a one-stop lean, but the banked section and 22 corners are an unknown until the cars run. Treat all strategy as provisional.
spots: Heavy braking off the main straight + DRS; the obvious primary passing spot.
One of the heaviest braking zones, after a quick section + DRS.
The banked sweep could enable closer following into the zones that follow.
otNote: No race history yet. The design pairs long full-throttle runs with heavy-braking zones and a banked sweep, which on paper should promote overtaking, but the inaugural weekend will tell the real story.
corners: Tight 90° sequence off the start straight + DRS. A likely first-lap flashpoint and the prime passing spot.
Quick direction change opening the lap.
A fast left after a long full-throttle run that gradually bends right. A real challenge.
Medium-speed corners linking to the banked section.
The banked stadium-style section, a rare feature on the F1 calendar.
The signature "La Monumental" sweep through the IFEMA grounds.
A technical mid-speed sequence setting up the heavy braking.
Quick flick before the lap’s biggest stop.
One of the heaviest braking zones on the lap; a key late-race overtaking chance.
Flat, forgettable 90° bends back onto the main straight.
geo: Debuting in 2026 around the IFEMA Madrid exhibition centre in Barajas, near the airport. A "show-circuit" in the Miami/Vegas mould, replacing Barcelona as the Spanish GP host.
Part street, part purpose-built, blending public-road sections with constructed track through the exhibition grounds.
Its headline feature is a banked stadium-style sweep, a rarity in F1 and a deliberate spectacle for the new venue.
With no prior running, every figure here, from sector times to strategy, is provisional until the cars hit the track in September.
